content: We can construct a UNPrompt-style zero-shot graph anomaly detection framework that leverages triple hybrid retrieval to sample candidate anomalous nodes from the unified input graph, directly enabling high-performance zero-shot node-level anomaly detection without task-specific fine-tuning. This approach treats different node subgraph contexts as the 'query corpus' for retrieval, combining three complementary node matching signals to surface out-of-distribution anomalous nodes that existing single-modality GAD methods miss.
source_topics: Zero-shot node generalization on unified graph
trigger_topic: Zero-shot node generalization on unified graph
